data.title: Arrowhead CFO Ken Myszkowski to retire, Daniel Apel to succeed
data.description:
• Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ARWR) said on Tuesday that Ken Myszkowski has notified the company of his decision to retire as Chief Financial Officer of the company, effective as of May 13, 2025. 
  • Myszkowski has served as the company’s CFO since 2009. Myszkowski’s decision to retire was not the result of any disagreement with the company.
  • Following the effective date, Myszkowski will serve as an employee adviser and provide transition services, as requested by the company, to facilitate an effective transition of his job responsibilities to his successor, until January 31, 2026. 
  • The company determined that it would appoint Daniel Apel to serve as CFO of the company, effective as of May 13, 2024, until the earliest of Apel's removal, termination, or resignation from such office.
  • Apel joins the company from Walgreens Boots Alliance, where he served as Global Head of Financial Planning and Analysis from 2019 through 2024.

data.title: Cymat receives approval for $1.3M private placement
data.description:
• Cymat Technologies (TSXV:CYM:CA) has received approval from the TSX for the non-brokered private placement of equity units for gross proceeds of $1.28M.
  • Cymat will issue 11,671,435 common shares and the same number of common share purchase warrants.
  • Each unit was priced at $0.11 with one unit consisting of one common share and one common share purchase warrant.
  • Each warrant entitles the holder to purchase one common share at a price of $0.13 for a twenty-four month period.
  • Proceeds of the offering will be used for capital equipment purchases in support of a major strategic business development initiative and for working capital requirements.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stocks falter as cautious traders monitor tariff developments; banks climb on earnings
data.description:
Wall Street on Tuesday ended slightly lower in a choppy trading session, as a boost from positive bank earnings petered out amid more caution over developments surrounding tariffs. 

The benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) fell -0.17% to conclude at 5,396.63 points. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slipped -0.05% to settle at 16,823.17 points, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) shed -0.38% to finish at 40,368.96 points. 

The Financial sector closed +0.2%, boosted by a +3.7% gain in Bank of America (BAC) and a +1.8% advance in Citi (C). The number two and number four U.S. bank by assets, respectively, delivered double-digit Y/Y percentage growth in first quarter net income. 

BofA top boss Brian Moynihan noted "resilience" in consumers who have continued to spend and maintain healthy credit quality. Meanwhile, Citi CEO Jane Fraser reckoned that after all the trade wars, "the U.S. will still be the world’s leading economy, and the dollar will remain the reserve currency."  

On the tariff front, Bloomberg News reported that the European Union (EU) and the U.S. had made little progress in trade negotiations, and that the EU expects the U.S. trade barriers to remain in place. Earlier, another Bloomberg report said China had directed its domestic airlines to stop taking delivery of new aircraft from U.S. planemaker Boeing (BA).

White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt in an afternoon press briefing said more than 15 trade deals were actively being considered, and that U.S. President Donald Trump wanted to personally sign off on them. On China, Leavitt quoted Trump as saying "the ball is in (the Asian nation's) court" in terms of making a trade deal.     

Traders have had to digest several - sometimes confusing - updates on tariffs since Friday, but the overall picture has been one of positivity, helping boost sentiment on Monday. 

"Markets have calmed, and the VIX has dropped below the 30 level. While trade barriers caused by tariffs will continue to cause periodic volatility, the next significant event could be Washington's progress on the administration's agenda. Extending the tax cuts could be critical for the stock market," Andrew Hecht, investing group leader of Hecht Commodity Report, told Seeking Alpha.  

"Meanwhile, the dollar index's decline below the 100 level has supported gold, which remains on a bullish path. However, the higher the price climbs, the greater the odds of a correction, as even the most aggressive bull markets rarely move in straight lines. Crude oil bounced back over the $60 level but remains in a bearish trend, with bullish and bearish factors pulling the energy commodity in opposite directions," Hecht added. 

Over in the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields stabilized after a bruising bond selloff in longer-term maturities last week. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was last down 4 basis points to 4.34%. The shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down marginally to 3.86%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: United Airlines expects resilient earnings in Q2, tops Q1 estimates
Interactive Brokers Group Non-GAAP EPS of $1.88 misses by $0.05, revenue of $1.43B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Interactive Brokers Group press release (NASDAQ:IBKR): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.88 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $1.43B (+19.2% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • Customer accounts increased 32% to 3.62 million.
  • Customer equity increased 23% to $573.5 billion.
  • Total DARTs2 increased 50% to 3.52 million.
  • Customer credits increased 19% to $125.2 billion.
  • Customer margin loans increased 24% to $63.7 billion.

data.title: Net Power COO, CFO to depart
data.description:
• Net Power (NYSE:NPWR) on Tuesday said Brian Allen, President and Chief Operating Officer, and Akash Patel, Chief Financial Officer, will no longer serve in their roles with the company.
  • Allen and Patel will officially depart the company on May 1.
  • The company has appointed Marc Horstman as COO, effective immediately.
  • Horstman previously served as Net Power’s Head of Product Development, joining the company in May 2023. Danny Rice, Net Power's CEO, will assume the title of President as well as serve as the company's interim CFO.
  • NPWR -0.66% after hours to $2.255.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Zuckerberg considered Instagram spinoff in 2018
data.description:
Mark Zuckerberg had thought of spinning off Instagram in 2018, according to a court document seen on Tuesday during the FTC trial against the social media and tech giant. 

The CEO of Meta had considered the move back then while contemplating the possibility of a future antitrust probe into his company.

"I wonder if we should consider the extreme step of spinning Instagram out as a separate company. As calls to break up the big tech companies grow, there is a non-trivial chance that we will be forced to spin out Instagram and perhaps WhatsApp in the next 5-10 years anyway," Zuckerberg had said in a memo that was seen at the trial in Washington, D.C.

During the trial, the Facebook founder admitted that he purchased Instagram because it had a "better" camera compared with the one he was trying to build, and his company failed several times to build in-house apps.

"We were doing a build vs. buy analysis. I thought that Instagram was better at that, so I thought it was better to buy them," he told the court.

"We probably tried building dozens of apps over the history of the company, and the majority of them don't go anywhere," he said.

The landmark FTC trial, which started on Monday, is aiming to undo Meta's acquisition of WhatsApp and Instagram from a decade ago on allegations of holding an illegal monopoly in the social media market.

The complaint submitted by the FTC said Zuckerberg's company put together a deliberate strategy to "eliminate threats to its monopoly." Meta has argued, saying that the FTC cleared the deals a long time ago, and it now competes with TikTok (BDNCE), YouTube (GOOG) (GOOGL), X, and iMessage (AAPL).

data.title: DOGE requests some SEC data access, but faces pushback - report
data.description:
Eliezer Mishory, a member of Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency, sought access to certain Securities and Exchange Commission data, including staff emails, contracts and payments systems, though his request was pushed back by Acting Chairman Mark Uyeda, Bloomberg reported Tuesday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

A former official from the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, Mishory has been representing DOGE at the SEC since joining earlier this month. He previously served as a top lawyer at prediction market Kalshi Inc.

Whether DOGE has secured access to any SEC data or why it wanted those records remains unclear, the article said, noting that the SEC declined to comment and Mishory did not immediately respond.

The SEC holds a massive amount of confidential data, from active probes to whistleblower details to live trading records. 

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Green Plains adds three new board members; BofA downgrades on macro headwinds
data.description:
Green Plains (NASDAQ:GPRE) -5.5% in Tuesday's trading after appointing three new board members, expanding the board to 10 members through the 2025 annual meeting, when two tenured directors are expected to step down, and saying has formed a strategeic planning committee to support efforts to enhance shareholder value.

The company also said it entered into a cooperation agreement with long-term shareholder Ancora Holdings, which includes provisions for a standstill and voting commitment.

Separately, Bank of America downgrades Green Plains (NASDAQ:GPRE) to Neutral from Buy with $4.50 price target, cut from $10 previously, as increased risks from a deteriorating macro environment and emerging trade war offset the potential for strong returns should the company’s initiatives succeed.

BofA's Salvator Tiano remains constructive on Green Plains' (GPRE) company’s carbon capture project, which could be "transformational," but notes several risks that could impair the company's valuation, including potentially significantly weaker ethanol margins as crude oil and gasoline prices decline, the demand for gasoline and thus ethanol blending drops, and the risk that other countries will impose tariffs on U.S. ethanol imports.

The current fiscal and macro landscape reduces the possibility that the 45Z tax credit would be extended, thus lowering the returns of the CC&S project; given Green Plains' (GPRE) vulnerable financial position, Tiano believes the increased uncertainty no longer justified a Buy rating.

data.title: First Farmers Financial announces up to $4M stock buyback program
data.description:
First Farmers Financial (OTCQX:FFMR) announced on Tuesday an up to $4M share repurchase program.

The program to buy back outstanding common stock in the open market will be funded from the currently available working capital.

As of March 31, the company had 6,999,207 common shares outstanding.
